gaygent_header: THE GAYGENT'S OFFICIAL SURVEILLANCE REPORT — Sweet 16 Preview
hot_take: The Round of 32 was a SLAUGHTERHOUSE and I loved every second of it. Florida — ONE-SEED Florida — is GONE. Kansas is GONE. Gonzaga, Kentucky, Vanderbilt, Virginia — GONE, GONE, GONE, and GONE. Twenty-six players across this pool are now collecting unemployment and three managers have fewer active players than I have functioning brain cells at 2 AM. Alex Magged was in EIGHTH PLACE five days ago. He is now in FIRST. AJ Spile has FIVE dead players and is somehow still in second. Jake Pollack has TWO living players. TWO. And we haven't even gotten to the good rounds yet. The Sweet 16 starts Thursday and The Gaygent has never been more ALIVE.
bust_roast: Solo Ball scored ZERO points in the Round of 32 for UConn. Zero. On a team that WON. Schaf's second-round pick is averaging 4.5 points per tournament game and contributing exactly nothing except a name I enjoy saying. Joshua Jefferson has 2 total points across two rounds for Iowa State — TWO — on a 16.9 PPG average, and Iowa State keeps WINNING which means Jordan Mackler has to watch his second-round pick get carried to the Sweet 16 like luggage on a conveyor belt. But the real bust redemption story? Nate Ament. Zero in R64. Everyone — including me — wrote the obituary. Then he dropped 16 in R32 for Tennessee like he was a completely different human being. Alex Magged's spreadsheet predicted this. I will not be asking how.

managers.Alex Magged: I am going to be PHYSICALLY ILL. Alex Magged was in EIGHTH PLACE after the Round of 64. Eighth. Dead last energy. A 144-point afterthought. And then the Round of 32 happened and this pencil-pushing NIGHTMARE scored 107 points — the highest R32 total in the entire pool — and vaulted to FIRST PLACE with 251 points. FIRST. I need to lie down. Trey Kaufman-Renn went from 25 to 44 and is quietly becoming the most reliable player in this tournament. Dailyn Swain — an 11-seed! — has 38 points on Texas. Morez Johnson is putting up 36 on 1-seed Michigan. David Mirkovic dropped 29 in R64 and added 7 more. Isaiah Evans scored 17 for Duke in R32 alone and has 33 total. Billy Richmond III — an EIGHTH-ROUND PICK — has 25 points on 4-seed Arkansas. And Nate Ament? The man I personally eulogized for scoring ZERO in R64? Scored 16 in R32 like he read my column and took it PERSONALLY. Seven of eight alive. The only casualty is Boogie Fland, who scored 23 total before Florida got bounced by a team I refuse to acknowledge. Alex has players on Duke (1), Michigan (1), Purdue (2), Illinois (3), Arkansas (4), Tennessee (6), and Texas (11). Seven teams across four regions. This is not a roster. This is a PORTFOLIO DIVERSIFICATION STRATEGY and I despise it and I despise HIM and his perfect disgusting spreadsheet that is absolutely going to win this pool.
managers.AJ Spile: AJ Spile is in second place with 248 points and the structural integrity of a building that passed inspection with three walls. FIVE of his eight players are eliminated. Five. BYU (Dybantsa), Missouri (Mitchell), Ohio State (Thornton), Louisville (Conwell), and TCU (Punch) — five teams, five funerals, and AJ is STILL only 3 points behind first. How? Because the three men standing are MONSTERS. Ja'Kobi Gillespie has 50 total points for Tennessee — 29 in R64, 21 in R32 — and shows zero signs of slowing down. Labaron Philon has 38 on Alabama. Milan Momcilovic has 37 on Iowa State. That's 125 points from three players who are all on teams seeded 2-6. But here's the nightmare math: if ANY of those three teams loses in the Sweet 16, AJ drops from a three-legged stool to a two-legged stool, and I have never seen a two-legged stool that didn't end with someone on the floor. AJ built a roster that front-loaded points and back-loaded risk and right now the risk is staring at him from across the table like it KNOWS something. Ryan Conwell scored 21 in R32 — Louisville's highest — and then Louisville lost and those 21 points became the most expensive goodbye gift in pool history. AJ Spile is 3 points from first place and one upset from oblivion. I am OBSESSED with this energy.
managers.Mark Weiner: Oh, MARK. My sweet, chiseled, SURGING Mark. You were fourth after R64. Now you're THIRD with 235 points and a R32 performance of 106 points that was second only to — and I am gagging as I say this — Alex Magged. Alex Karaban is having the tournament of his LIFE: 22 in R64, 27 in R32, 49 total, the second-highest individual score for any active player in the pool. UConn keeps winning and Karaban keeps eating. Meleek Thomas has 40 on Arkansas. Malik Reneau has 40 on Miami FL — a 7-seed that keeps dancing and keeps making Mark look brilliant. Aday Mara added 16 in R32 for Michigan and sits at 35 total on the tournament's 1-seed. And CAYDEN BOOZER. The Cayden Boozer experience continues — 9 more in R32, 28 total, a 294% overperformance relative to his 7.1 PPG season average. This man is scoring at nearly TRIPLE his regular season rate in the tournament and Mark is the only manager unhinged enough to have drafted him. The casualties? Thomas Haugh put up 19 for Florida in R32 — scored MORE in the loss than he did in the R64 win — before Florida got sent home. That's 33 dead points from a first-round pick. But Mark has five players alive across Duke (1), Michigan (1), UConn (2), Arkansas (4), and Miami FL (7). Those arms are carrying this portfolio to the Sweet 16 and I am NOT looking away.
managers.Jake Pollack: The Toyota Camry has been TOTALED. Rolled. Set on fire. Insurance called. Claim denied. Jake Pollack has TWO active players. Two out of eight. A 25% survival rate. The Round of 32 was an EXTINCTION EVENT for this roster: Alex Condon scored 21 for Florida before Florida died, Thijs De Ridder scored 22 for Virginia before Virginia died, Christian Anderson had 7 for Texas Tech before they died, and Tre White had 4 for Kansas before they died. Four players eliminated in one weekend. Jake went from six alive to two alive in 48 hours. The two survivors? Coen Carr on Michigan State with 38 total points and Emanuel Sharp on Houston with 34. That's it. That's the whole operation. Two players. Two different teams. Two different regions. Every remaining point Jake scores for the rest of this tournament comes from exactly two human beings. If Michigan State or Houston loses in the Sweet 16, Jake becomes a one-stock portfolio, and a one-stock portfolio in March is just a man sitting alone at a party watching everyone else dance. Jake scored 93 in R32 — a respectable number! — but 72 of those points came from players who are now DEAD. Jake Pollack is the most tragic 221-point total I have ever witnessed and I genuinely feel something close to empathy, which for me is basically love.
managers.Brandon Nye: Brandon 'Analytics Don't Lie But They Do Hurt' Nye is in fifth with 212 points and the unique distinction of having his HIGHEST-SCORING PLAYER eliminated. Tyler Tanner scored 53 total points — 26 in R64, 27 in R32 — the second-highest individual total on any roster in this pool — and then Vanderbilt LOST and all 53 of those points became a monument to what could have been. A MAUSOLEUM of production. Brandon's four living players — Wagler (32 on Illinois), Hopkins (31 on St. Johns), Stirtz (29 on Iowa), and Flemings (27 on Houston) — are all scoring consistently but none of them are stars. They're role players on tournament teams, which is either perfectly sustainable or deeply mediocre depending on how generous you're feeling. The dead weight column reads like a support group: Blackwell (22, Wisconsin), Wright III (14, BYU), Johnson (4, Akron). Half the roster eliminated. But here's what the analytics actually say: Brandon has players on Houston (2), Illinois (3), St. Johns (5), and Iowa (9). Three of those four teams are seeded 5 or lower. If the chalk holds in the Sweet 16, Brandon's portfolio contracts. If the upsets continue? The 9-seed Iowa Hawkeyes and 5-seed St. Johns are exactly the kind of teams that either die Thursday or become the story of the tournament. Brandon read the analytics. The analytics said 'good luck.'
managers.Jordan Mackler: The PROPHECY was fulfilled. I said it in the Round of 64 report — Iowa State vs Kentucky meant Jordan's Jefferson vs Jordan's Oweh, and one of them had to die. Iowa State won. Kentucky lost. Otega Oweh — who scored 35 in R64 and 18 in R32, 53 TOTAL POINTS — is eliminated. The self-cannibalization happened and it ate the WRONG player. But here's why Jordan Mackler is the most fascinating roster in this pool: Darius Acuff Jr has SIXTY POINTS. Six-zero. Twenty-four in R64, THIRTY-SIX in R32. The highest individual total for any player in the entire pool, active or dead. A first-round pick performing like a first-round pick for the first time in this draft's history. Acuff alone has more points than three of Mike Haughey's players COMBINED. And Jordan still has SEVEN players alive — tied with Alex for the most in the pool. Pryce Sandfort (38) on Nebraska, Ivan Kharchenkov (23) on Arizona, Jeremy Fears (19) on Michigan State, Patrick Ngongba (4) on Duke, Joshua Jefferson (2) on Iowa State, and Braylon Mullens (0) on UConn. Yes — Mullens has scored zero points in two rounds for a team that keeps winning. Zero. Jefferson has TWO. But Jordan has players on Arizona (1), Duke (1), UConn (2), Iowa State (2), Michigan State (3), Arkansas (4), and Nebraska (4). Seven teams, four regions, two 1-seeds, two 2-seeds. The low-end deadweight is being offset by Acuff playing like a man possessed and the DEPTH of this roster is terrifying. Jordan Mackler is in sixth place and climbing and I am obsessed with this trajectory in a way that is frankly becoming unprofessional.
managers.Mike Haughey: I WARNED him. I said the double-exposure on Arizona, St. Johns, and Florida was either brilliant insurance or a house of cards. FLORIDA COLLAPSED AND TOOK TWO OF HIS PLAYERS WITH IT. Xaivian Lee scored 17 in R32 and then Florida went home — 27 total dead points. Urban Klavzar added 5 more before joining Lee in the afterlife — 16 total dead points. And Graham Ike — the first-round pick, the 19.7 PPG alpha — scored 25 in R32 for Gonzaga, bringing his total to 44, the highest on Mike's roster, and then Gonzaga LOST. That's 87 points from three dead players. EIGHTY-SEVEN. But the house of cards still has two walls standing: Mike's Arizona double (Koa Peat 29, Motiejus Krivas 20) is alive on the 1-seed, and his St. Johns double (Zuby Ejiofor 32, Oziyah Sellers 14) is alive on the 5-seed. Elliot Cadeau quietly scored 12 in R32 for Michigan and has 17 total. Five players alive. 199 points total — tied with Jordan for sixth. The long game I mocked in the R64 report is actually producing results now: Mike scored 105 in R32, the THIRD-highest total in the pool, after being dead last after R64. If Arizona makes the Elite Eight, Mike gets TWO players' worth of points from every game. That's the kind of leverage that turns a seventh-place manager into a dark horse. I hate that I'm saying this about Mike Haughey but I'm saying it: he matters now.
managers.Schaf Chulay: Schaf. My DARLING. My obsession. What happened to you this weekend was a HATE CRIME against beauty. Kansas lost and took Darryn Peterson — your BEST PLAYER, 49 points, the man whose injury history solved itself in R64 — AND Flory Bidunga (18 points) with it. Two players on one team, both dead. Then Virginia lost and Malik Thomas (23 points) went with them. And Nolan Winter was already dead from Wisconsin in R64. FOUR of eight eliminated. Fifty percent survival. From seven alive to four alive in one weekend. The wreckage includes 98 points of dead production — nearly HALF of Schaf's 197 total. But here's why I refuse to give up on this man: Tamin Lipsey scored 26 in R32 after scoring 3 in R64. THREE to TWENTY-SIX. That's an 767% increase and if that's not a character arc I don't know what is. Brayden Burries has 34 on 1-seed Arizona. Milos Uzan has 27 on 2-seed Houston. And Solo Ball has... 9 points. Nine. Solo Ball scored zero in R32 for a UConn team that WON. But his name is Solo Ball and I will root for him until this tournament pries him from my beautiful manicured hands. Four players alive on Arizona (1), Iowa State (2), Houston (2), and UConn (2). Three 2-seeds and a 1-seed. If the chalk holds, all four play in the Sweet 16 and Schaf is still in this. The obsession continues. Diminished. Wounded. But ALIVE.
managers.Josh Ehrlich: Josh Ehrlich went from 'present but not particularly engaged' to 'accidentally competent' and I need everyone to appreciate the trajectory. 185 points. Five alive. Three dead. The dead: Rueben Chinyelu (14, Florida — scored zero in R32 before Florida collapsed), Sam Lewis (17, Virginia), and Tyler Bilodeau (0, UCLA — still hasn't scored a single point in this tournament, which at this point feels like performance art). But the living roster? Yaxel Lendeborg scored TWENTY-FIVE POINTS in R32 for Michigan after scoring 9 in R64. He went from an afterthought to the second-highest scorer on Josh's roster with 34 total. Andrej Stojakovic went from 9 to 30 — 21 in R32 for Illinois. Jaden Bradley scored 18 in R32 for Arizona and has 25 total. Braden Smith has 38 on Purdue. Trevon Brazile has 27 on Arkansas. Five players across Arizona (1), Michigan (1), Purdue (2), Illinois (3), and Arkansas (4). Three of them are on 1 or 2 seeds. Josh scored 89 in R32 — middle of the pack — but the COMPOSITION of his surviving roster is quietly excellent. He went to Michigan. Michigan is a 1-seed. He has a player on Michigan. I refuse to acknowledge whether that's relevant. Josh Ehrlich is in ninth place and I genuinely believe he doesn't care, which is either enlightened detachment or the same lack of competitive instinct that made him bet against the group in the Miami-Indiana incident. One of those.
managers.Nick Reskin: Nicky. Sweet, distant, too-cool-for-this-pool Nicky. You are in LAST PLACE with 149 points and the Cameron Boozer situation has gone from 'mystery' to 'tragedy.' Duke has now played TWO tournament games. Cameron Boozer — the NPOY candidate, 22.5 PPG, the number TWO overall pick in this draft — is still showing 0 points, seed 0, team blank in the data. Cayden Boozer has 28 points for Mark Weiner on the same Duke team. If Cameron Boozer's points were properly tracked, Nick's total would be... well, I'm not going to do Alex Magged's job for him, but it would be SIGNIFICANTLY different. The rest of the roster: Tarris Reed Jr had a quiet 10 in R32 after dropping 31 in R64 — he's at 41 total and still UConn's engine but the engine is idling. Fletcher Loyer EXPLODED for 24 in R32 on Purdue and has 38 total — the bright spot. Jaxon Kohler added 10 on Michigan State for 22 total. But the dead pile is mounting: Amani Lyles (26, Akron), Duke Miles (22, Vanderbilt — another R32 casualty), Tyon-Grant Foster (0, Gonzaga), and Terrance Hill Jr (0, VCU). Four eliminated, three of them scored either nothing or modest totals. Nick has four active players on UConn (2), Purdue (2), Michigan State (3), and... whatever team Cameron Boozer plays for. If the Boozer data resolves, Nick jumps. If it doesn't, Nick finishes last and we all pretend the draft was rigged. The old Nicky would have fought this. The new Nicky is probably too busy being an adult. I miss the old Nicky so much it ACHES.
